# flashblade_array_connection (Resource)

Manages a FlashBlade array connection to a remote FlashBlade array.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "flashblade_array_connection" "example" {
  remote_name        = "remote-fb"
  management_address = "10.10.0.1"
  connection_key     = var.array_connection_key
  encrypted          = true

  replication_addresses = ["10.10.1.1", "10.10.1.2"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `remote_name` (String) The name of the remote array. Used as the import identifier. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `connection_key` (String, Sensitive) Connection key of the remote array. Required when creating a new connection. Write-only: not returned by GET. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `encrypted` (Boolean) Whether data is encrypted in transit.
- `management_address` (String) Management IP or hostname of the remote array. Required when creating a new connection, computed for imported/passive-side connections.
- `replication_addresses` (List of String) Replication IP addresses or FQDNs.
- `throttle` (Attributes) Bandwidth throttle configuration for the array connection. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--throttle))
- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the array connection.
- `os` (String) Operating system of the remote array.
- `status` (String) Connection status (connected, connecting, etc.).
- `type` (String) Connection type (async-replication, etc.).
- `version` (String) Version of the remote array.

<a id="nestedatt--throttle"></a>
### Nested Schema for `throttle`

Optional:

- `default_limit` (Number) Default bandwidth limit in bytes per second.
- `window_end` (String) End time of the throttle window (HH:MM format).
- `window_limit` (Number) Window bandwidth limit in bytes per second.
- `window_start` (String) Start time of the throttle window (HH:MM format).


<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
- `read` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Read operations occur during any refresh or planning operation when refresh is enabled.
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import an existing array connection by remote array name.
terraform import flashblade_array_connection.example remote-fb
```
